record_identity.title: Distributed governance concepts for STEWARD-domain instruments

summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments to incorporate distributed governance concepts that clarify authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability across multi-actor stewardship models.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Existing STEWARD instruments assume centralized stewardship assumptions; explicitly encoding distributed authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability reduces hidden cartel risk, preserves challenge pathways, and improves plural reviewability and resilience of governance processes.
proposal_rationale: STEWARD-domain instruments need concepts for distributed authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability so multi-actor stewardship arrangements remain reviewable and resistant to capture.

source_records.relevance_note: Proposal content and discussion captured in Caelestis Access Module.

primary_artefact_access.access_status: not independently re-assessed during registry-wide metadata migration
primary_artefact_access.access_method: existing VIGIL source package and record content
limitations: This metadata pass does not retroactively claim direct inspection of every historical primary source.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Assessment is limited to the evidence actually accessible to the named reviewer and must not be read as direct audiovisual verification unless primary_artefact_access states otherwise.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments with distributed governance terms, expected behaviors, and operational controls for authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, contestability, and reviewability.
automation_components: future checks for stewardship routing metadata
interface_components: registry display of stewardship governance concepts

suggested_insertion_points: Identify target STEWARD clauses for distributed governance terminology.
Draft controls for plural reviewability and capture resistance.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to governance designers, standards communities, regulators, and platform operators working with multi-actor stewardship, accountability, contestability, and capture-risk controls.

next_action: Revise STEWARD-domain instruments to define distributed governance concepts and operational controls: 1) define terms and expected behaviors for distributed authority (multiple actors), distributed legitimacy (plural reviewability), distributed dependence (hidden cartel risk), distributed capture (coordinated asymmetry), and distributed contestability (preserved challenge pathways); 2) add requirements for transparency, rotation, and external review to mitigate capture and dependence; 3) specify detection and remediation protocols for cartel-like coordination; 4) incorporate mapping to OPERATIONS taxonomy and repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) where provenance or reliance implications exist; 5) publish an implementation timeline and compatibility guidance for existing STEWARD instruments.
migration_notes: V2 fields added by additive migration; legacy flat fields preserved for compatibility.
Normalized to the clean VIGIL record design: source_records is the only canonical source-evidence block in individual records; source_data is not used.
